reasoning: The Lean signature mirrors the informal statement component-by-component. The finite set A with |A|>1 corresponds to α being a Fintype with Fintype.card α > 1. "G is a permutation group on A" corresponds to G : Subgroup (Equiv.Perm α). The transitivity hypothesis ∀ a b, ∃ σ : G, σ(a) = b is the standard correct encoding of a transitive action. The conclusion ∃ σ : G, ∀ a, σ(a) ≠ a is exactly "there exists a derangement in G." G's finiteness is automatic since it is a subgroup of the finite group Equiv.Perm α, matching the implicit finiteness in the informal statement. No hypotheses are added or dropped.
